lesson: A leak at the bowl rear with a healthy auger shaft seal is the bowl gasket — a cheap routine wear item, almost never the bowl itself.
symptoms: Sticky red syrup pooling under the machine.
Leak traced to the back of the left bowl.

tests.inspect-beater: The rear of the left bowl is wet — a bead of syrup tracking down from the gasket line. The shaft seal above it is dry.

symptoms: Staff put a takeaway tub under the left bowl. It fills daily.
The drip is coming from behind the bowl, not the tap.
Blue raspberry stain spreading across the counter overnight.
Level in the left bowl drops even with the tap unused.

flavour: Cleaned up a strawberry crime scene for the price of a gasket.
authenticityNote: Bowl gaskets are a routine wear item on slushie machines; a leak at the bowl rear is almost never the bowl itself. Tier-1 cousin of the soft-serve door O-ring — trivially cheap once you actually look.
